programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve's undercover surveillance operation is hampered by news that his suspect has been deliberately infected with the plague. Dick Van Dyke and Barry Van Dyke star

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Returning to port with their catch, Richard Kimble and tuna boat owner Tony Donovan find a strike against the cannery in progress - with the police on their way

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry Mason solves the murder of a double-crossing ship captain and uncovers a scheme to defraud an insurance company

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The ranger gets involved when a vengeful group of police officers are revealed to be murdering criminals who are set free on technicalities. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A roller derby star is killed during a game, but it is unclear which side the culprit belongs to. Meanwhile, an author is found dead with the final chapter of his novel scrawled all over his body, leading the detectives to conclude he may have suffered from a compulsive urge to write. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A traumatised marine escapes from a psychiatric unit, convinced he is still fighting in Iraq. The agents believe his erratic behaviour indicates post-traumatic stress disorder and try to calmly talk him round before restraining him. However, blood tests reveal high levels of steroids in his system - meaning someone is using him as a human lab rat. Starring Lauren Holly, Sean Murray and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen has been placed on suspension after going rogue and shooting dead the Chameleon, leaving the once tight-knit team shaken and divided. Meanwhile, former operations manager Hetty, who quit over her colleague's actions, is adjusting to life in retirement. Crime drama set in the high-stakes world of the Office of Special Projects, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J and Linda Hunt

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble, rescued from a sheriff's posse by a motorcycle gang, is set up as a fall guy in a murder

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A comedian tries to take the blame when the woman he loves is accused of murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed in what initially seems to be a road-rage incident - until investigations reveal evidence of foul play. Digging deeper, the team finds the dead man belonged to an underground fight club, and sets about discovering whether his involvement was a factor in the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An experimental dairy farm comes under Walker's scrutiny when it looks like his friends at the Cherokee Federal Reservation are being put at risk. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a former marine killed during a drone strike on a terrorist target - but with the victim's family refusing to believe he would have returned to the Middle East willingly, NCIS officers try to determine why he was associating with a notorious Taliban bomb-maker. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, the team works overtime to solve a robbery - a case made doubly difficult by the fact that the prime suspect is a former petty officer who has supposedly been dead for 17 years. Eventually arrested at a Vietnam memorial, he manages to convince Gibbs of his innocence - but a discovery in the forensics lab suggests he may be guilty after all. Guest starring Peter Coyote

programme.desc: Calum cooks a meal, but it takes so long to prepare his guests drink too much and pass out before it is ready. Meanwhile, James encounters a dog that faints to gain attention. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and John McGlynn

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Tinker is arrested for possessing erotic antiques and tries to wriggle out of it by implicating Lovejoy in a robbery. Meanwhile, Charlotte gets involved with a dodgy pawnbroker whose auction of unclaimed property contains some very choice lots indeed. Starring Ian McShane, Dudley Sutton and Caroline Langrishe

programme.title: French Fields

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: The duo investigate the murder of a high-profile judge in the midst of a prominent drugs trial, but Pascoe is distracted by an important personal decision which, for once, his partner cannot help him with. Crime drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad investigates a long-running feud between two family ice-cream businesses, which resulted in a shooting and several armed raids 10 years ago. Standing enlists the aid of his daughter in cracking the case, and she discovers evidence of drug dealing - but Pullman does not approve of her involvement. With Dennis Waterman and Amanda Redman, alongside guest star Jodie Kelly

programme.desc: Part one of two. Jacob is shocked to learn his lawyer wife Naomi (guest star Kim Thomson) has been mugged in a dark car park. It emerges this was not a random attack but the work of someone with a personal grudge seeking revenge. The search begins for Bobby Henshaw, whose younger brother was knocked down and killed by Alistair Finch, who walked away scot-free from court after Naomi successfully defended him

programme.desc: Heledd Cynwal, Iestyn Jones and Siôn Tomos Owen explore Dyffryn Nantlle, discovering the history of the towns and villages that grew out of the slate industry

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford take on the fraudsters leaving people thousands of pounds out of pocket, look at the big-name companies who have left some of their customers hanging and present expert advice to help make sure consumers get the best deal available

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow a four-year project to develop land in Maidstone, Kent, plus properties in Burnley and Meir, Staffordshire

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The team investigate a wealthy manager who has been taking advantage of the NHS, and the million-pound tax fraudster who claimed to be a spy

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A teenager fights to save her father's life when he has a heart attack, and a driver loses control of a car, scooping two men up on to the bonnet and crashing through a shop window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Caroline Hawley guide two teams scouring Spitalfields Market in the heart of London's East End for bargains to take to Catherine Southon's auction house. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out how the area around Spitalfields became known as Weaver's Town with TV historian Dan Cruickshank

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid makes preparations for the big apple Sale, while Karen is excited by the prospect of another foster child. Luca gets involved with a patient undergoing superhero therapy

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Helena, Andy, Carly and Tony vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A musical couple from London need Steve Brown's help to unearth a new home in Oxfordshire. On the £950,000 shopping list is a detached property with space for a studio and good links to the capital. Away from the search, Steve checks out a dairy farm where he samples kefir, purportedly an ancient, health-giving drink made from fermented milk

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves an old radio, a set of chairs and a side table and chest of drawers from being thrown in the tip, hoping he and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The antiques rivals arrive in Suffolk, where Tim Medhurst likes the look of an art nouveau piece, while Irita Marriott learns why the local cottages are pink

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is shocked to see Katy at the club and feels conflicted when her mother asks her for a character reference. Martin is annoyed by Zack's constant flirting with Ruby - and even more annoyed when Lily tells him the pair of them spent a lot of time together while he was away. Bernie is under pressure to come up with money for her family and a mystery man arrives in the Square looking for Sonia. Brian Conley joins the cast

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A major incident strikes Holby and with pupils from Hanssen's old school fighting for life, Josh worries that the CEO is struggling to cope. Will he be brave enough to ask for help before it's too late? Secret addict Kian's focus is frayed when asked to choose between a patient and surrogate son Andrei, and inexperienced doctor Jeong is thrown in at the deep end when Dom asks him to diagnose a mystery condition

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam in which a woman ended up £20,000 out of pocket after beginning an online relationship with a US Navy captain

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: As the police begin interviewing the brewery workers about Jack's background and his behaviour at the party, the women go to extraordinary lengths to protect their dark secret. However, someone else knows what happened that night and is now holding them to ransom. Drama, starring Laura Fraser, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Eiry Thomas and Heledd Gwynn, with Eddie Marsan and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening matches in the penultimate round of Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City at Stamford Bridge. Chelsea and the Foxes were facing each other for the second time in four days, having also met in the FA Cup final, and this contest could have proved to be just as important as the clubs jostled for positions inside the top four. Plus, Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City, Manchester United v Fulham and Southampton v Leeds United

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The amateur make-up artists are booked for a photo shoot for online retailer ASOS. They are given two hours to showcase their mascara, but also feature a shimmery eye and a glow on the skin, all of which must align with the company's brand identity. Back in the studio, the amateurs create a look that is 'out of this world', before the two weakest face off in the elimination. Presented by Maya Jama

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma turns to Romero for guidance when she finds herself in an unexpected position of power. As the pair push the limits with the powerful Bob Paris, Norman and Dylan both come undone at the fallout of a secret, while Caleb considers an offer made to him by a surprising source

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Emma grows closer to Dylan when they both go to Norman's aid. Meanwhile, Norma begins to consider a life away from White Pine Bay

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Gloria Hunniford and Angela Rippon tackle viewers' consumer bugbears, from poor customer service to delayed refunds and shoddy goods. Plus, more advice from some of the country's leading experts on everything from money worries to holiday conundrums

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a semi-detached in Sittingbourne, Kent, a cottage in Landrake in Cornwall and a flat in London's Maida Vale

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The fraud investigators hunt a reckless hoaxer faking accidents for fun and a high-living couple claiming benefits while dodging hundreds of thousands in tax

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young boy rushes for help after his grandfather falls down a cliff face, a road racing cyclist's career is threatened when he is hit by a car and a motorbike rider hits black ice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey help two teams scour an antiques fair in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, while presenter Anita Manning catches up with series regular Charles Hanson to discuss a very special musical collection

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The day of the big apple sale is here, but Valerie cannot seem to concentrate on it. Sid has to deal with a patient who is looking to get back on the dating scene

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Ewan, Chelsea, Mandy and Gav

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a newlywed couple in Shropshire, who have £400,000 to buy a country home that will give them room in the future for a growing family. While in the county, Jules visits Weston Park, an estate shaped by the historic pioneer of landscape design, Lancelot 'Capability' Brown

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her fellow upcyclers restore a broken games table, a pair of old lamps and a tired display cabinet

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott journey through Bedfordshire, where they learn the history of British airships and take the opportunity for Irita to drive a 120-year-old tractor

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on what happens when care goes wrong in hospitals, revealing how some NHS trusts seemingly keep critical reports hidden from the regulator and the public. Repeated scandals have shown how transparency is vital to make sure lessons are learned. However, after a year-long investigation uncovering around 100 unpublished independent reports, reporter and GP Faye Kirkland discovers how hospital failings are apparently being buried

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Will Kirk brings his expertise to bear on a carved walnut piano stool which has met with an untimely accident, leaving it with only three legs. Horologist Steve Fletcher's knowledge of dials, numbers and needles is called upon to resurrect a broken speedometer from 1920s motor car, and basket-maker Sarah Hatton works on a wicker baby crib that has fallen into disrepair. Finally, Brenton West and Will join forces to restore a 19th-century tippling stick - a walking stick with a hidden tube for whisky inside - thought to have once belonged to King George V

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewers take part in reduce, reuse and recycle week, in which all the fabric in the haberdashery is replaced with charity shop clothes and soft furnishings. They begin by following a pattern for a gentleman's waistcoat, followed by a transformation challenge in which they change items of army surplus into a stylish garment for a woman. Finally, they must create a made-to-measure dress using old jeans, before the sixth sewer is asked to leave. Judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant make that decision, while Joe Lycett presents

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park. Liverpool were looking for revenge after Burnley ended the Reds' 68-match unbeaten home run with a 1-0 win in January's reverse fixture, while a close match was anticipated between the Eagles and Arsenal given the last three matches between the sides had ended in draws. Plus, Everton v Wolverhampton Wanderers, Newcastle United v Sheffield United, Tottenham Hotspur v Aston Villa and West Bromwich Albion v West Ham United

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: The entrepreneurial spirit among the young, spearheading an economic fightback, featuring high-end Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos Parpa who decided to 'cook his way' out of Covid by working with his business partners to create a pop-up 'restaurclub', where customers can eat, drink and dance at the same time. Plus, Toni unveils her latest member of staff at Banter Cards - six-month-old Spencer, who now models Banter's brand-new baby range

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker looks back at highlights of the 50th series of the quiz show so far, featuring team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell alongside guests including Ugo Monye, Jade Jones, Stuart Pearce, Osi Umenyiora and Sam Quek

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals. Angela Rippon, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford introduce free expert advice on issues from IT to banking and everything in between, as well as viewers' emails and messages answered live on the show

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of properties in Leeds, Sidcup in Kent, and Pontygwaith in south Wales

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Fraud specialists investigate a devious dentist fleecing the NHS to pay for holiday homes and luxury cars, and a con-artist making thousands by pretending to know US politician Hilary Clinton. Plus, the race to stop £17million of unpaid tax going up in smoke. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles recounts the story of a base jumper who leapt from a cliff top, only fort his parachute to spin him 180 degrees into a rock face. Plus, the college students injured after their coach left the road and turned over

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper help two teams in Hungerford, Berkshire, while Eric Knowles visits Sandham Memorial Chapel to find out about local war artist Stanley Spencer

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid's colleagues try to support him after his tragic loss, Luca struggles with a dilemma, and Valerie and Nathan's day in the woods gives her food for thought

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Neil, Malcolm, Emma and Nicola vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester for three unwanted items he can save and sell on. Furniture restyler Chloe Kempster attempts to give an old tallboy a new lease of life, while woodworker Bruce Faulseit has an interesting challenge on his hands with a collection of slate tiles. For his own project, Jay has a trio of metal lights to overhaul

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott tour Somerset, acquainting themselves with public houses along the way, and Irita unexpectedly finds something to buy that's hanging on the wall

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rocky tells Sonia stories from her childhood, but when she remains unimpressed he goes on to regale the customers in the Vic with tales of his various escapades. Mick comes clean to Linda about his statement, an angry Martin confronts Zack, and Isaac's condition worries his family and friends

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Plant-able children's books are the offering from two friends hoping to gain a Dragon to grow their brand, and a pair of ex-bankers pitch their ambitious at-home private chef service. A Wirral-based entrepreneur pitches his range of luxury home fragrance products, while a husband-and-wife duo are keen to broaden out their scent-detection courses for dogs. Whether multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies will smell any potential profit remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories, with the odds stacked against children from the West Indies. Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey with experts Nell Wragg and Sarah Peterson, who restore an unwanted windsurf sail, a chest of drawers, and a child's cot

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Newbie Irita Marriott brings Latvian flair to the game as she takes on Tim Medhurst in a sporting tour of the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire. One expert has a brush with the law

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In Adam Frost's garden, he gives his tips on lawn repair, grows some unusual vegetables and plants a seasonal container. Meanwhile, Advolly Richmond brings us up to date in her history of lawns

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: To celebrate the depth of talent among the traders and customers, the team puts on a talent show, while a new trader arrives at the market with a surprising idea for making money

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Matt's insistence on finding the mother who gave Georgie up for adoption has unforeseen consequences for himself and Olivia. When top surgeon Neil Burton arrives at Inverness Hospital it becomes immediately obvious that he is not there for Henry's job. Anna has a shock for Mrs Collins

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown uncovers the historic secrets of seabirds in Yorkshire, Gillian Burke finds out sea creatures become stressed, and Chris Packham meets a couple adding to shark science

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Nick Davies scour the crowd at Tilbury Docks in Essex to find objects to take off to auction, while Paul Martin explores the history of the docks

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh go head to head testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Hassocks and makes his way to the beautiful Sussex village of Ditchling, where a Roman Catholic community of artists once made their home. Next, he visits the seaside town of Bexhill-on-Sea, before the Kent and East Sussex heritage railway conveys Michael from Bodiam Castle through the scenic Rother Valley to Tenterden, and finally arrives in the village of Benenden

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The eight regional champions cook their fish courses as they battle to win a place on the menu of a banquet celebrating British innovation. Engineer and TV presenter Zoe Laughlin joins the judging panel of food critic Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and food creative Rachel Khoo, to decide who should make it to the banquet

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: It's the grand final, and the three finalists return to the workshop to compete to be crowned the winner. They are each challenged to make two pieces of truly outstanding jewellery - pair of pearl earrings and a bridal headpiece. Who will the judges decide is worthy of the title Britain's next big jewellery star?

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented time, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines. University Hospital Coventry's gynaecological oncology department deals with rising referrals, a growing backlog and complex cases as the hospital's theatre capacity attempts to return to normal

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Lucy Porter, Frankie Boyle and Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'next' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The journalist interviews the renowned actress about her life and career, including her award-winning performances in TV series Little Dorrit, the film Age of Innocence, and more recent appearance as Professor Sprout in the Harry Potter movies

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Navy veteran David, who survived an attack on the SS Atlantic Conveyor in May 1982, during the Falklands war. David is desperate to reconnect with his fellow crewmen. Plus, the story of Darryl, an author whose family came to the UK as part of the post-war wave of migration from former British colonies. He searches for the teachers who helped him overcome childhood racism and xenophobia. Anita Rani presents

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. Meanwhile, when 18-year-old Samahit leaves for university, his family travel with him to bless his new home at the halls of residence

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick heads into the county's china clay pits to discover what is known locally as white gold, one of its most important industries. He then climbs the Cornish Alps, huge slag heaps created by the clay mining and some of Cornwall's highest land. After a wild swim at one of Cornwall's picturesque hidden coves, Rick cooks a simple sea bass recipe, revealing how to make the perfect mayonnaise. In the far west of the county, on the Land's End peninsula, Rick meets his niece, a modern artist, who explains her deep spiritual connection to Cornwall

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion is in Leominster in Herefordshire, where she learns about the history of the local hat and glove industries. Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri are helping the teams

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The antiques rivals arrive in Suffolk, where Tim Medhurst likes the look of an art nouveau piece, while Irita Marriott learns why the local cottages are pink

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Self-made millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies observe a pitch by a plucky entrepreneur with lofty ambitions for his genetically personalised meal shakes. A married couple hope their alcoholic gift box business will make for an intoxicating proposition and the Dragons' are put to the test with a virtual escape room business - will they unlock their coffers and strike a deal? Finally, a 'head nit-picker' is itching to show off her headlice treatment and clinic. Whether the Dragons share her passion for parasites remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces highlights from the latest round of matches in Super League, as supporters return to stadiums for the first time since March 2020. Fixtures include St Helens v Salford Red Devils, Leigh Centurions v Wigan Warriors, Warrington Wolves v Huddersfield Giants and Hull FC v Catalans Dragons

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: Market boss Frank puts on an ambitious event to celebrate the market's deep roots in the East End. Meanwhile, a young entrepreneur arrives at the market with a strange new idea

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Matt and Elizabeth exchange some home truths, while Sarah and Jack are concerned about Stan O'Rouke's behaviour, which is becoming more and more aggressive

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. To shake things up, however, the judges present their tasks in a different order to normal. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown makes a jaw-dropping discovery of sharks' teeth on the beach. Plus, Chris Packham meets a photographer who believes the UK has the best marine life in the world

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire, where antique experts Jonathan Pratt and Adam Partridge find a First World War pigeon capsule and a large collection of toy cars

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo follows his 1930s Bradshaw's guide to the unspoilt East Sussex port of Rye, where he learns about the loss of a generation of lifeboatmen in 1928. On the beaches at Rye, Michael explores one of 28,000 pillboxes constructed around the British coastline during the Second World War, before boarding the Romney, Hythe and Dymchurch railway to Dungeness

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift reveal the trendsetters amongst the designers and growers at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show, and discover who has influenced our gardening habits. Elsewhere, fashion designer Paul Smith shares what inspires him at the show year after year

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This time, the eight regional champions cook their main courses in a bid to secure a place on the menu of a banquet celebrating British innovation. With the most complex dishes of the week to prepare, and only two spots left on the banquet menu, the chefs are pushed to their limits, and guest judge Carol Vorderman joins food critic Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Rachel Khoo as they pick the dish they want to see at the banquet

programme.title: The Psychedelic Drug Trial
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling a ground-breaking new trial at Imperial College London, which sees a psychedelic drug tested against an antidepressant as a treatment for depression. Filmed over 16 months, the programme follows a team of scientists and psychotherapists as they compare the effects of psilocybin, the active ingredient of magic mushrooms, with an antidepressant on a small group of participants with clinical depression

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by Larry Dean and Gary Delaney

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented time, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines. University Hospital Coventry's gynaecological oncology department deals with rising referrals, a growing backlog and complex cases as the hospital's theatre capacity attempts to return to normal

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of award-winning novelist Kazuo Ishiguro, who discusses his early life in Nagasaki and howe growing up in the home counties in the 1960s shaped his viewpoint as a writer. He also discusses his youthful desire to be a singer-songwriter, and chats about the music that has had a particular impact on his work. With contributions from writers Hanya Yanagihara and Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray set about fixing, feeding and repairing the lawns, while Kirsty Wilson creates a new living lawn and Brian Cunningham encourages people not to mow if possible. Chris Beardshaw has updates on the projects he started in his home garden last year

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the post-war British modern art movement in the seaside town of St Ives, the home for leading artists such as Barbara Hepworth and Terry Frost. At the Lizard Peninsula, Rick heads out to sea to fish for red mullet, and on the wild coast at Land's End, he discovers why Cornwall has a rather unusual animal as its national emblem

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott journey through Bedfordshire, where they learn the history of British airships and take the opportunity for Irita to drive a 120-year-old tractor

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts as the five remaining contestants are challenged to make a pair of identical gold hoop earrings, before creating a gold locket for a real-world client. Who will impress the judges enough to stay to make it through to the semi-final?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: The inaugural Dagenham Sunday Market Food Awards take place, and two new traders show off their wares - edible cakes for dogs and wooden chainsaw sculptures

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Having suffered flashbacks from her near-fatal experience with Stan O'Rourke, Sarah finds the strength to embrace her happiness with George

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter H, visiting the Hyacinth National Collection, before Monty Don demonstrates how to plant the bulbs. Plus, a look at keeping hens

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. To shake things up, however, the judges present their tasks in a different order to normal. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: How doing the laundry is harming marine life, why the fish-and-chip supper has changed and which lobster is making a comeback. With Steve Brown, Gillian Burke and Chris Packham

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Old College in Aberystwyth, Raj Bisram is intrigued by a 19th-century sewing box, and Christina Trevanion is wowed by a sapphire and diamond ring. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin visits a Regency restoration project at the National Botanic Garden of Wales

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From the Kent Cinque Port of Deal, Michael Portillo heads to Walmer Castle, home during the 1920s to Lord Beauchamp, whose openly homosexual lifestyle led to his exile from the country. And in Margate, Michael discovers the connection to TS Eliot

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift celebrate the positive power of plants as they focus on the designers and growers striving for a better world with the creations they bring to the event. They reveal how, over the years, the show has contributed to the better promotion of the health of the planet, plants and people, and talk to Countryfile presenter Ellie Harrison and BBC correspondent Fergal Keane about the importance of gardens to mental wellbeing

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Comedian Ed Gamble joins food critic Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and cook and broadcaster Rachel Khoo to select the menu's final flourish - the dessert course. This is the last chance the chefs have of craft a course at the banquet celebrating British innovation, so tensions are running high as the rivals cook against each other for the final time. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Identical twin brothers George and Peter who fled Nazi Germany in 1939 search for the family of the man who helped them escape. Plus, Clare, who housed 21 child refugees from wartorn Bosnia, looks for their mystery schoolgirl translator Petra, whose language skills allowed the children to properly communicate and share the horrors they had fled. Presented by Anita Rani

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and series regular Hugh Dennis poke fun at recent news in the company of Catherine Bohart, Ed Byrne, Darren Harriott, Rhys James and Felicity Ward

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: It is dinner at Trent Manor, and everyone around the table has a motive for murdering Lord Trent. But who did? Mandy wasn't invited to the manor, so she is off doing something else. Comedy short, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with guest star David Bradley

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: A look at the emergence of Steve Davis and the rise of promoter Barry Hearn's Matchroom group of players, which brought lucrative sponsorships into the game. By the mid-1980s, snooker was at the peak of its powers and in 1985 nearly 20 million people tuned in to see Steve Davis play Dennis Taylor in the World Championship final

programme.title: The Psychedelic Drug Trial
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling a ground-breaking new trial at Imperial College London, which sees a psychedelic drug tested against an antidepressant as a treatment for depression. Filmed over 16 months, the programme follows a team of scientists and psychotherapists as they compare the effects of psilocybin, the active ingredient of magic mushrooms, with an antidepressant on a small group of participants with clinical depression

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: Makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores what the beauty looks of three iconic moments in British history reveal about that era, beginning with the high Georgians. Lisa goes to Chatsworth House in Derbyshire in search of one of the beauty icons of the day, Georgiana, Duchess of Devonshire, and discovers how in this period of extreme wealth in Britain, the rich entered an arms race of beauty. Later, she recreates an authentic Georgian look on a 21st century model

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants go global, taking on garments and techniques from around the world. For the pattern challenge they make a French breton top - and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly matched stripes and a completely flat neck - before they transform sarongs into completely different garments. In the final round, the sewers must come up with an outfit inspired by Mexican artist Frida Kahlo, known as much for her personal style as for her vivid artwork. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwall's most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the county's oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic, and Robbie helps out a stranded boater

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas a lone cabin nestled in a snow-covered landscape

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest 2021
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal, Scott Mills and Chelcee Grimes commentate on the opening semi-final from Rotterdam, which sees the first 16 countries competing for a place in Saturday's grand final. The show is opened by the Netherlands' Duncan Laurence, the most recent winner from 2019, before the hopeful nations - Lithuania, Slovenia, Russia, Sweden, Australia, North Macedonia, Ireland, Cyprus, Norway, Croatia, Belgium, Israel, Romania, Azerbaijan, Ukraine and Malta - take to the stage

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Science writer Steven Johnson and historian David Olusoga embark on an investigative journey through 300 years of scientific breakthroughs and go behind the scenes of modern medicine to meet the unsung heroes tackling Covid-19 and other public health threats in today's world. They begin by examining how the development of vaccines has helped to prevent the spread of serious disease and how new ones are developed when never-before-seen diseases emerge

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The vitamin industry is worth more than £400million a year in the UK alone, but what are these pills actually doing for people's health? Dr Giles Yeo aims to find out, exploring if vitamin and mineral supplements are what they appear to be and putting people's diets to the test to find out if they really need these supplements. Those he meets include a scientist in Copenhagen, whose groundbreaking research into antioxidants indicates they may not have the extraordinary health-enhancing properties that people believe, and a nutrition scientist from the University of Greenwich, who tells Giles that on average most people in the UK are meeting their vitamin requirements thorough their diet

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how scientists exploited the natural world to make medicines, beginning by investigating the challenges they faced trying to create a drug that would alleviate pain. The programme examines what physical discomfort actually is, why people want to control it, and how the discovery of morphine led to 200 years of scientific breakthroughs and self-experimentation

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: First in a five-part series in which Dr Adam Rutherford investigates the close relationship between discoveries in anatomy and the works of art that illustrate them. He begins by focusing on Roman anatomist Claudius Galen and artist and part-time dissector Leonardo da Vinci

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas a lone cabin nestled in a snow-covered landscape

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic, and Robbie helps out a stranded boater

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Science writer Steven Johnson and historian David Olusoga embark on an investigative journey through 300 years of scientific breakthroughs and go behind the scenes of modern medicine to meet the unsung heroes tackling Covid-19 and other public health threats in today's world. They begin by examining how the development of vaccines has helped to prevent the spread of serious disease and how new ones are developed when never-before-seen diseases emerge

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming's exploration of the English canal network continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more tips for arts enthusiasts as he transforms a zany-looking canvas into a soft woodland scene, with a radiant sun glowing over opulent trees and bushes

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Air traffic controllers guide a passenger jet with a major technical fault safely to the ground, before responding to an unidentified flying object detected on radar screens. Meanwhile, British Airways Captain Paul Bonhomme takes part in an air race at Ascot, and a YouTube blogger realises he must undertake special training for his new camera-drone. Plus, a woman tries to overcome her fear of flying, and a man sets out to prove that he can learn to fly, despite having restricted growth

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on wartime Hull and the consequences of a specific bomb that fell on the city's 6th Avenue, flattening two houses and changing the lives of two families for ever. The Taylors lost baby Peter and 14-year-old Vera was very badly burned. Next door, Mrs Owens had put three of her children under the stairs for safety, but they didn't survive. Contributors include Mrs Owens' daughter Janet, who was born after the war and has many questions about what happened to her siblings. Here she meets the surviving Taylor sisters to talk about the event

programme.title: Cold War, Hot Jets
programme.desc: Britain emerged from the Second World War in financial crisis, but the development of the jet engine provided hope for the future and resulted in a world-leading aviation industry. In the first of a two-part documentary, historian and author James Holland examines how the jet became a lucrative export and a powerful piece of military hardware during the Cold War, and looks at how selling to the wrong buyer could alter the balance of power

programme.title: Classic British Cars: Made in Coventry
programme.desc: To celebrate the award of UK City of Culture 2021 to Coventry, local boy Mark Evans tells the full history of the classic cars made in Coventry. This passionate retrospective runs from the industry's beginnings in 1897 through to the present day and includes interviews and archive footage detailing the stories behind the creation of classic marques such as the Daimler, the Triumph, the Riley and the Jaguar and the companies and people behind them

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: The police launch a huge search for William, suspecting he's been taken by the killer, while Peter Vinge tries to celebrate Johannes's birthday without Masja. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: As the police prepare a public appeal to find new leads on the killer, Alice offers a reward on social media for information. Louise's boyfriend comes to visit her. In Danish

UK viewers have the opportunity to vote for their favourite, while the UK entry, James Newman, joins the action from the Netherlands

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Docudrama portrait of Delia Derbyshire, the electronic sound pioneer behind the Doctor Who theme tune, exploring the idea that this extraordinary composer herself lived outside of time and space. Delia's story is told through two archives: the first, a collection of lost works, 267 reels of quarter-inch magnetic tape recordings of Delia's work found in her attic after her death, the other, her school books, paintings and keepsakes, discovered in her childhood bedroom

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

All the parents and babies have different toys or objects that feel fluffy too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a safe, baby-appropriate fluffy toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to explore with their baby. Some viewers at home show what fluffy items they’ve been playing with too. The traditional nursery rhyme Mary Had a Little Lamb features, as does a story about Baby Bear exploring different textures. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

Betty sets off to find out where the noise is coming from. She bumps into Fred the Pill Millipede, who is practicing his tap dancing. Is that the noise? Or maybe it’s Sid the Snail practicing his meditation? Betty needs to find out if she’s ever to get her little ones to sleep.

After heading home with their families, Samuel plays in his swing with mum. Matilda has a soothing, warm bath before being breastfed and rocked slowly to sleep. Samuel is also getting tired, so mum and dad run him a warm bath to make sure he gets cleaned up, before giving him his bottle of milk. The next morning, Matilda’s family are all in bed together as she gives Callum a big hug! Samuel is also feeling refreshed and ready to have some cuddles with mum before breakfast.

He solves a puzzle for Harry and Henry the Hedgehogs and encourages Brie the Bumblebee to get warm so she can fly. Brie is able to return the favour and points him in the right direction to meet Sid. We join Etti, who is having a fun day out with her family at some local gardens. They see lots of different flowers and animals including a big rabbit statue! But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.desc: Arthur Sleep is joined by team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt in this slapstick quiz show featuring guests and clips from Gigglebiz. Joining Gail and Keith for this GiggleQuiz are Wizard Tripwick and Captain Adorable. Round 1 is a picture round featuring hidden objects in photos of DIY Dan and Professor Muddles. Gail’s team turn to the Crystal Ball for help. In Nana’s Panto Showdown, Nana Knickerbocker and her cows are busy making pizzas. After the Crystal Ball refuses to help, Gail’s team are still yet to score a gigglepoint at the halfway point in the show. Round 3 features a clip starring King Flannel trying to get a biscuit. After some more refusal from the Crystal Ball, Wizard Tripwick and Gail struggle to answer the questions themselves. Keith produces his own crystal football to help his team with the answers. After saying goodbye to the Crystal Ball and totting up the final scores, the winners of this GiggleQuiz are revealed.

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: A loud-mouthed, abrasive American comes to stay at the hotel and soon exposes Basil's ignorance of transatlantic cuisine - particularly Waldorf salad. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: As the police begin interviewing the brewery workers about Jack's background and his behaviour at the party, the women go to extraordinary lengths to protect their dark secret. However, someone else knows what happened that night and is now holding them to ransom. Drama, starring Laura Fraser, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Eiry Thomas and Heledd Gwynn, with Eddie Marsan and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening matches in the penultimate round of Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City at Stamford Bridge. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on what happens when care goes wrong in hospitals, revealing how some NHS trusts seemingly keep critical reports hidden from the regulator and the public. Repeated scandals have shown how transparency is vital to make sure lessons are learned. However, after a year-long investigation uncovering around 100 unpublished independent reports, reporter and GP Faye Kirkland discovers how hospital failings are apparently being buried

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Will Kirk brings his expertise to bear on a carved walnut piano stool which has met with an untimely accident, leaving it with only three legs. Horologist Steve Fletcher's knowledge of dials, numbers and needles is called upon to resurrect a broken speedometer from 1920s motor car, and basket-maker Sarah Hatton works on a wicker baby crib that has fallen into disrepair. Finally, Brenton West and Will join forces to restore a 19th-century tippling stick - a walking stick with a hidden tube for whisky inside - thought to have once belonged to King George V

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewers take part in reduce, reuse and recycle week, in which all the fabric in the haberdashery is replaced with charity shop clothes and soft furnishings. They begin by following a pattern for a gentleman's waistcoat, followed by a transformation challenge in which they change items of army surplus into a stylish garment for a woman. Finally, they must create a made-to-measure dress using old jeans, before the sixth sewer is asked to leave. Judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant make that decision, while Joe Lycett presents

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park. Liverpool were looking for revenge after Burnley ended the Reds' 68-match unbeaten home run with a 1-0 win in January's reverse fixture, while a close match was anticipated between the Eagles and Arsenal given the last three matches between the sides had ended in draws. Plus, Everton v Wolverhampton Wanderers, Newcastle United v Sheffield United, Tottenham Hotspur v Aston Villa and West Bromwich Albion v West Ham United

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: The entrepreneurial spirit among the young, spearheading an economic fightback, featuring high-end Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos Parpa who decided to 'cook his way' out of Covid by working with his business partners to create a pop-up 'restaurclub', where customers can eat, drink and dance at the same time. Plus, Toni unveils her latest member of staff at Banter Cards - six-month-old Spencer, who now models Banter's brand-new baby range

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of properties in Leeds, Sidcup in Kent, and Pontygwaith in south Wales

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Fraud specialists investigate a devious dentist fleecing the NHS to pay for holiday homes and luxury cars, and a con-artist making thousands by pretending to know US politician Hilary Clinton. programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid's colleagues try to support him after his tragic loss, Luca struggles with a dilemma, and Valerie and Nathan's day in the woods gives her food for thought

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Neil, Malcolm, Emma and Nicola vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester for three unwanted items he can save and sell on. Furniture restyler Chloe Kempster attempts to give an old tallboy a new lease of life, while woodworker Bruce Faulseit has an interesting challenge on his hands with a collection of slate tiles. For his own project, Jay has a trio of metal lights to overhaul

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott tour Somerset, acquainting themselves with public houses along the way, and Irita unexpectedly finds something to buy that's hanging on the wall

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rocky tells Sonia stories from her childhood, but when she remains unimpressed he goes on to regale the customers in the Vic with tales of his various escapades. Mick comes clean to Linda about his statement, an angry Martin confronts Zack, and Isaac's condition worries his family and friends

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Plant-able children's books are the offering from two friends hoping to gain a Dragon to grow their brand, and a pair of ex-bankers pitch their ambitious at-home private chef service. A Wirral-based entrepreneur pitches his range of luxury home fragrance products, while a husband-and-wife duo are keen to broaden out their scent-detection courses for dogs. Whether multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies will smell any potential profit remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories, with the odds stacked against children from the West Indies. Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics
