Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford take on the fraudsters leaving people thousands of pounds out of pocket, look at the big-name companies who have left some of their customers hanging and present expert advice to help make sure consumers get the best deal available

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow a four-year project to develop land in Maidstone, Kent, plus properties in Burnley and Meir, Staffordshire

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The team investigate a wealthy manager who has been taking advantage of the NHS, and the million-pound tax fraudster who claimed to be a spy

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A teenager fights to save her father's life when he has a heart attack, and a driver loses control of a car, scooping two men up on to the bonnet and crashing through a shop window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Caroline Hawley guide two teams scouring Spitalfields Market in the heart of London's East End for bargains to take to Catherine Southon's auction house. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out how the area around Spitalfields became known as Weaver's Town with TV historian Dan Cruickshank

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid makes preparations for the big apple Sale, while Karen is excited by the prospect of another foster child. Luca gets involved with a patient undergoing superhero therapy

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Helena, Andy, Carly and Tony vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A musical couple from London need Steve Brown's help to unearth a new home in Oxfordshire. On the £950,000 shopping list is a detached property with space for a studio and good links to the capital. Away from the search, Steve checks out a dairy farm where he samples kefir, purportedly an ancient, health-giving drink made from fermented milk

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves an old radio, a set of chairs and a side table and chest of drawers from being thrown in the tip, hoping he and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The antiques rivals arrive in Suffolk, where Tim Medhurst likes the look of an art nouveau piece, while Irita Marriott learns why the local cottages are pink

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is shocked to see Katy at the club and feels conflicted when her mother asks her for a character reference. Martin is annoyed by Zack's constant flirting with Ruby - and even more annoyed when Lily tells him the pair of them spent a lot of time together while he was away. Bernie is under pressure to come up with money for her family and a mystery man arrives in the Square looking for Sonia. Brian Conley joins the cast

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A major incident strikes Holby and with pupils from Hanssen's old school fighting for life, Josh worries that the CEO is struggling to cope. Will he be brave enough to ask for help before it's too late? Secret addict Kian's focus is frayed when asked to choose between a patient and surrogate son Andrei, and inexperienced doctor Jeong is thrown in at the deep end when Dom asks him to diagnose a mystery condition

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam in which a woman ended up £20,000 out of pocket after beginning an online relationship with a US Navy captain

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: As the police begin interviewing the brewery workers about Jack's background and his behaviour at the party, the women go to extraordinary lengths to protect their dark secret. However, someone else knows what happened that night and is now holding them to ransom. Drama, starring Laura Fraser, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Eiry Thomas and Heledd Gwynn, with Eddie Marsan and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: From Bobby Storey's funeral to protests like Black Lives Matter, the response to Covid-19 posed unexpected questions about the Police Service of Northern Ireland. As society emerges from lockdown restrictions, Jennifer O'Leary investigates how the pandemic - along with the Irish Sea border - has exposed tensions over policing

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening matches in the penultimate round of Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City at Stamford Bridge. Chelsea and the Foxes were facing each other for the second time in four days, having also met in the FA Cup final, and this contest could have proved to be just as important as the clubs jostled for positions inside the top four. Plus, Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City, Manchester United v Fulham and Southampton v Leeds United

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Morgan is desperate to know if ex Josh wanted an open relationship. With the truth finally out could they have a future together? Tasheeka thought she'd found her perfect match in Dexter until he suddenly ghosted her. He's ready to reveal the truth but will it be too much to swallow? Childhood sweethearts Daniel and Scarlett were plain sailing until he left her to go travelling, and may not be able to rebuild a friendship

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma turns to Romero for guidance when she finds herself in an unexpected position of power. As the pair push the limits with the powerful Bob Paris, Norman and Dylan both come undone at the fallout of a secret, while Caleb considers an offer made to him by a surprising source

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Emma grows closer to Dylan when they both go to Norman's aid. Meanwhile, Norma begins to consider a life away from White Pine Bay

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Gloria Hunniford and Angela Rippon tackle viewers' consumer bugbears, from poor customer service to delayed refunds and shoddy goods. Plus, more advice from some of the country's leading experts on everything from money worries to holiday conundrums

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a semi-detached in Sittingbourne, Kent, a cottage in Landrake in Cornwall and a flat in London's Maida Vale

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The fraud investigators hunt a reckless hoaxer faking accidents for fun and a high-living couple claiming benefits while dodging hundreds of thousands in tax

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young boy rushes for help after his grandfather falls down a cliff face, a road racing cyclist's career is threatened when he is hit by a car and a motorbike rider hits black ice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey help two teams scour an antiques fair in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, while presenter Anita Manning catches up with series regular Charles Hanson to discuss a very special musical collection

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The day of the big apple sale is here, but Valerie cannot seem to concentrate on it. Sid has to deal with a patient who is looking to get back on the dating scene

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Ewan, Chelsea, Mandy and Gav

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a newlywed couple in Shropshire, who have £400,000 to buy a country home that will give them room in the future for a growing family. While in the county, Jules visits Weston Park, an estate shaped by the historic pioneer of landscape design, Lancelot 'Capability' Brown

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her fellow upcyclers restore a broken games table, a pair of old lamps and a tired display cabinet

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott journey through Bedfordshire, where they learn the history of British airships and take the opportunity for Irita to drive a 120-year-old tractor

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Gavin Andrews turns his hand to the traditional craft of thatching as he helps the restoration of an old fisherman's cottage in Co Down. Jo Scott travels to Rathlin Island to meet a family using the ocean's resources for their business and Ruth Sanderson takes a dip as she learns about the growing popularity of sea swimming. Wildlife expert Conor McKinney finds out about badger baiting and the work being carried out to prevent the crime

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Will Kirk brings his expertise to bear on a carved walnut piano stool which has met with an untimely accident, leaving it with only three legs. Horologist Steve Fletcher's knowledge of dials, numbers and needles is called upon to resurrect a broken speedometer from 1920s motor car, and basket-maker Sarah Hatton works on a wicker baby crib that has fallen into disrepair. Finally, Brenton West and Will join forces to restore a 19th-century tippling stick - a walking stick with a hidden tube for whisky inside - thought to have once belonged to King George V

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewers take part in reduce, reuse and recycle week, in which all the fabric in the haberdashery is replaced with charity shop clothes and soft furnishings. They begin by following a pattern for a gentleman's waistcoat, followed by a transformation challenge in which they change items of army surplus into a stylish garment for a woman. Finally, they must create a made-to-measure dress using old jeans, before the sixth sewer is asked to leave. Judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant make that decision, while Joe Lycett presents

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and tweet

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park. Liverpool were looking for revenge after Burnley ended the Reds' 68-match unbeaten home run with a 1-0 win in January's reverse fixture, while a close match was anticipated between the Eagles and Arsenal given the last three matches between the sides had ended in draws. Plus, Everton v Wolverhampton Wanderers, Newcastle United v Sheffield United, Tottenham Hotspur v Aston Villa and West Bromwich Albion v West Ham United

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: The entrepreneurial spirit among the young, spearheading an economic fightback, featuring high-end Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos Parpa who decided to 'cook his way' out of Covid by working with his business partners to create a pop-up 'restaurclub', where customers can eat, drink and dance at the same time. Plus, Toni unveils her latest member of staff at Banter Cards - six-month-old Spencer, who now models Banter's brand-new baby range

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals. Angela Rippon, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford introduce free expert advice on issues from IT to banking and everything in between, as well as viewers' emails and messages answered live on the show

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of properties in Leeds, Sidcup in Kent, and Pontygwaith in south Wales

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Fraud specialists investigate a devious dentist fleecing the NHS to pay for holiday homes and luxury cars, and a con-artist making thousands by pretending to know US politician Hilary Clinton. Plus, the race to stop £17million of unpaid tax going up in smoke. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles recounts the story of a base jumper who leapt from a cliff top, only fort his parachute to spin him 180 degrees into a rock face. Plus, the college students injured after their coach left the road and turned over

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper help two teams in Hungerford, Berkshire, while Eric Knowles visits Sandham Memorial Chapel to find out about local war artist Stanley Spencer

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid's colleagues try to support him after his tragic loss, Luca struggles with a dilemma, and Valerie and Nathan's day in the woods gives her food for thought

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Neil, Malcolm, Emma and Nicola vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester for three unwanted items he can save and sell on. Furniture restyler Chloe Kempster attempts to give an old tallboy a new lease of life, while woodworker Bruce Faulseit has an interesting challenge on his hands with a collection of slate tiles. For his own project, Jay has a trio of metal lights to overhaul

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott tour Somerset, acquainting themselves with public houses along the way, and Irita unexpectedly finds something to buy that's hanging on the wall

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rocky tells Sonia stories from her childhood, but when she remains unimpressed he goes on to regale the customers in the Vic with tales of his various escapades. Mick comes clean to Linda about his statement, an angry Martin confronts Zack, and Isaac's condition worries his family and friends

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Plant-able children's books are the offering from two friends hoping to gain a Dragon to grow their brand, and a pair of ex-bankers pitch their ambitious at-home private chef service. A Wirral-based entrepreneur pitches his range of luxury home fragrance products, while a husband-and-wife duo are keen to broaden out their scent-detection courses for dogs. Whether multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies will smell any potential profit remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories, with the odds stacked against children from the West Indies. Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.desc: Mark Carruthers reviews the week's political news, inviting comment and analysis from Stormont and Westminster

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The amateur make-up artists are booked for a photo shoot for online retailer ASOS. They are given two hours to showcase their mascara, but also feature a shimmery eye and a glow on the skin, all of which must align with the company's brand identity. Back in the studio, the amateurs create a look that is 'out of this world', before the two weakest face off in the elimination. Presented by Maya Jama

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive

programme.desc: David Dickinson presents the show from Cheltenham racecourse, where items brought in to be valued include a pistol, a cluster ring, and a car boot cabinet

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five postal workers from Morecambe show their knowledge of top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants - Alan, Kiera, Sue and Gary - pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chas steps in to help Liv, Kim makes a difficult phone call, and it remains to be seen whether Gabby will be rumbled

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: The presenter makes his way to Seville in southern Spain, where he visits the San Leandro convent, where nuns have been selling homemade sweets to visitors for 400 years. Then, he finds the perfect spot for his cookbook kitchen, where he whips up a charred orange and spiced almond salad. Later, Ainsley learns about all things Iberico ham from deli owner Elio, before heading back to his pop up kitchen to whip up a sandwich

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: Martin Lewis returns with a live one-hour special edition of the show as we head into summer with loosening lockdown and potential holidays abroad. programme.title: Innocent

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: The staff prepare to let their hair down at their annual gala, where they celebrate their most profitable year and busiest season. It's also where the prestigious Front of House star award will be announced - and concierge Dean, who has been nominated, practises his dance moves in between dealing with guests. Meanwhile, Savoy Grill director Thierry prepares for another visit from owner Gordon Ramsay and quality executive Debra does her daily rounds to check things are in perfect condition. But there is a crisis looming as news of Covid-19 reaches the hotel

Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A policeman's murder gives Wycliffe plenty to think about - especially as the chief suspect is a fellow officer who has been having an affair with the victim's wife. Adding to the pressure, senior figures within the force want the matter cleared up as quickly as possible to avoid a scandal. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.desc: Nick tells Gail that Leanne wants a divorce. Sally is devastated by her mother's death, but Kevin is more interested in selling off her house. Jim returns home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: An anonymous enemy goes all out to ruin Wycliffe by sending the police a batch of photos apparently showing him taking bribes. As soon as she hears the news, Lane puts an investigation into the deaths of two drug-dealers on hold and leaps to her boss's defence - risking her career in the process. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a series of shootings claims one of their own, Ronnie and Joe have little time to grieve - they must solve the case before the killer reaches their next target on a hit list that includes CPS director Henry Sharpe. It gradually becomes apparent that the common thread connecting all the names is the case of Mark Glendon, convicted of the murder of his wife four years earlier. Although he's still in custody, a retrial is imminent, putting pressure on the team to catch the murderer before the jury delivers its verdict

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The leader of a censorship campaign comes to Oxford on a speaking tour, and Morse is charged with protecting her in light of a series of death threats. A bricklayer working on a controversial pop singer's house is later found dead, and when a second killing occurs, a connection emerges to the speaker's movement, plunging Morse into the middle of a conflict over the nature of British culture

programme.title: Law & Order: UK

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The severed arm of a missing environmental health inspector is washed up on the shores of the Humber and Frost's investigation leads him to a local meat factory. programme.title: That's My Boy

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George celebrates his 24th wedding anniversary by presenting Mildred with a carriage clock. However, he has purchased it cut-price from a dubious source, and when the Fourmiles return from holiday they find their home burgled, and among the missing items is a carriage clock. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A policeman's murder gives Wycliffe plenty to think about - especially as the chief suspect is a fellow officer who has been having an affair with the victim's wife. Adding to the pressure, senior figures within the force want the matter cleared up as quickly as possible to avoid a scandal. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick has to go undercover following the unlikely event of a gangster setting up shop in rural Aidensfield - and luckily his former girlfriend Jill is able to help out

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham fears for Kathy's safety after Biff's display of violence. Emily is excited about her date with Butch. Biff and Kathy have a heart-to-heart about their relationship

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gavin makes an amorous advance towards Stella. Butch takes Emily to see her dad in hospital. Kathy tells Graham she is determined to marry Biff

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice urges Leanne to fight for her marriage. Les accuses Nick of being unfaithful to Leanne. Des wants Samantha to have his baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick tells Gail that Leanne wants a divorce. Sally is devastated by her mother's death, but Kevin is more interested in selling off her house. Jim returns home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: An anonymous enemy goes all out to ruin Wycliffe by sending the police a batch of photos apparently showing him taking bribes. As soon as she hears the news, Lane puts an investigation into the deaths of two drug-dealers on hold and leaps to her boss's defence - risking her career in the process. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a series of shootings claims one of their own, Ronnie and Joe have little time to grieve - they must solve the case before the killer reaches their next target on a hit list that includes CPS director Henry Sharpe. It gradually becomes apparent that the common thread connecting all the names is the case of Mark Glendon, convicted of the murder of his wife four years earlier. Although he's still in custody, a retrial is imminent, putting pressure on the team to catch the murderer before the jury delivers its verdict

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.desc: The team is enlisted to track down a mystery plane responsible for destroying an American aircraft. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A woman hires Holmes and Watson to investigate the strange death of her sister, whose enigmatic final words seemingly hold the key to the case. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The duo investigate the strange deaths of several astronomers whose bodies have been mysteriously bleached white, and encounter an eccentric society dedicated to establishing contact with aliens from Venus. Jon Pertwee guest stars in the series' first colour episode

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is faced with a terrifying campaign of explosions and assassinations, apparently committed by an opposing operative that Cowley is convinced he killed several years earlier. It appears that he is either mistaken or an old associate of his former adversary is seeking revenge. Gordon Jackson stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry protects a widowed garage owner from her grasping family and Arthur spots a chance to achieve one of his greatest ambitions - getting his hands on a Rolls-Royce. Lionel Jeffries and Keith Barron guest star alongside Dennis Waterman and George Cole

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: Craig, Richard and Sharron investigate the disappearance of three Nemesis agents on a tropical island. Fantasy drama, starring Stuart Damon, William Gaunt and Alexandra Bastedo

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel to the Rio Ebro in Spain, where they target specimen roach and carp, and face the challenge of catching a giant wels catfish in a test of their skill and strength. They are joined on the final leg of their journey by globetrotting angler Gary Newman

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A valuable gem is stolen from the Countess of Morcar, leading the famous detective into a bizarre investigation involving a recovered Christmas goose. Mystery, starring Jeremy Brett, David Burke and Ken Campbell

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Mrs Peel investigate a spate of murders, and discover a ruthless businessman has been killing his rivals to gain a competitive edge. Vintage adventures, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are pitted against KGB assassins when a CI5 operative is apparently raised from the dead. A proposed exchange finds Cowley at a deserted aerodrome, and a burst of gunfire apparently leaves his life on the line. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry gets a job chauffeuring the villainous Micky the Fish, but fears for his life when the gangster's mistress reports her jewels missing. Light-hearted crime caper, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel across Italy, with their main target a monster wels catfish of up to 300lbs in the River Po. They also aim to catch smaller channel catfish from the River Arno and carp from a stunning mountain lake near Bologna

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Chat-show host Jonathan Ross, comedienne and writer Katy Brand, Watchdog's Chris Hollins and model Jo Wood compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh asks the questions

programme.title: Octopussy (1983)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates the mysterious death of a British agent in East Berlin, a mission that leads him to a glamorous, international jewel thief. He discovers one of her criminal cohorts, an Indian gangster, is in league with a crazed Soviet general who plans to heat up the Cold War by detonating a nuclear bomb in an American Air Force base. Spy adventure, with Roger Moore, Maud Adams, Steven Berkoff and Louis Jourdan

programme.desc: A former FBI agent is coaxed out of retirement to track down a serial killer behind the slaughter of entire families. Michael Mann's thriller, based on Thomas Harris's novel Red Dragon, with William Petersen, Dennis Farina, Tom Noonan, Brian Cox and Joan Allen

Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at a married couple that have made a booming business out of their hand-made Scotch eggs, and how a cotton mill-turned-soap factory is producing traditional bars, according to a process passed down through the generations. Plus, the team of craftsmen making the most accurate compasses in the world, and a behind-the-scenes look at the family firm making teddy bears that are cherished by children around the world

programme.title: The Blues Brothers (1980)
programme.desc: Two criminal brothers discover the orphanage where they were raised faces closure unless the nuns who run it can pay their tax bill, so the pair embark on an urgent mission to revive their old blues band to raise the money needed. Comedy, starring John Belushi, Dan Aykroyd, Aretha Franklin, James Brown, Ray Charles, Cab Calloway, Carrie Fisher, John Candy and John Lee Hooker

programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Presenters Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding, along with judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, welcome four more celebrities into the tent to expose their soft centres. Broadcaster Jeremy Paxman, comedian Joe Wilkinson, actress Sally Lindsay and reality star Georgia Toffolo make characterful biscuits for their signature challenge, followed by a technical that leaves them nowhere to hide, and finally they make a cake in the shape of their favourite hobby. They're baking to inspire viewers to do the same and raise money for Stand Up to Cancer

programme.title: The Money Maker
programme.desc: Entrepreneur and investor Eric Collins heads to Birmingham to visit frozen meal delivery company Winny's Meals. Shaun Sookoo started his Caribbean-inspired food company three years ago when he decided to turn his mum's home-cooked meals into a business and had friends queuing round to block to buy them. The business has been expanding at an exponential rate but when Eric looks at the figures, he is shocked to discover serious problems. Not only are customers no re-ordering, but Shaun's mother is not involved any more

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A romp through the archive of the unconventional dating show, beginning with assorted 'oh my god!' moments, and catching up with some of the most famous participants. They include Judith, the breast cancer survivor from series five who played The Lord Is My Shepherd to the potential dates and made them a Victoria sponge

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including SAS: Who Dares Wins, Dragons' Den, Three Families, Headspace: Guide to Sleep, Secret Crush, Lego Masters USA and This Morning. They also proffer their thoughts on a BBC News report about taking holidays abroad as the pandemic rules on travel are relaxed

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the restaurants he tried to save, Lido, Anna Vincenzo's and Le Bistro, to find out whether his advice has been put to good use

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: After a physically and psychologically intense introduction to their SAS selection, the obstacle for the 17 remaining recruits to overcome over the next 48 hours is fear, as they are sent deeper into the wild and unforgiving terrain of the Isle of Raasay in Scotland. They are put through their paces in a forward abseil, exposed to extreme cold in a water immersion exercise and required to operate as a team in a pitch-black disused tunnel

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The penultimate episode sees Sarah Kendall, Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack and Mike Wozniak all still in with a chance of winning Greg Davies's splendid gilded head. Lee has a makeover, Jamali gets destructive with some rubble, and Mike warns Greg's assistant Alex Horne about some potentially dangerous dairy produce. Meanwhile, Sarah is still the one to catch

programme.title: The Crystal Maze
programme.desc: Actress Tamzin Outhwaite takes on the role of captain as she and former cricket star Phil Tufnell, Dr Ranj Singh, reality star Francis Boulle and comedian Joel Dommett tackle the maze. Richard Ayoade is their guide as they face an assortment of skill, physical, mystery and mental challenges, where their aim is to win as much time as possible in the Crystal Dome

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets Sam, a 30-year-old conservation worker from Somerset who suffers from spinal muscular atrophy, and helps to design a fully accessible mobile home that will allow him to move freely inside as well as taking it on the road to travel the country. He also discovers how a former pub landlord is building his new girlfriend a surprise housewarming gift - a pub in their back garden - and views a modernist tent on the New Zealand island of Waiheke. Plus, George and Will Hardie search for engineering inspiration for their futuristic rotating home

programme.title: The £100k Drop

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: An offhand comment to the twins' new friend has dire consequences for Ray, who cannot understand why everyone is so upset - but he compounds his error by accidentally repeating the remark to the boy's father. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

Comedy, starring Peter Boyle, Doris Roberts, Ray Romano and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy chats non-stop through a basketball game, and Ray responds by suggesting to Robert that she talks too much - a criticism she does not take well. Comedy, starring Monica Horan and Ray Romano

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Attractive and popular psychologist Dr Honey Snow dates Frasier, who is so flattered that he agrees to write the introduction to her latest book - a decision that later causes him a king-size crisis of conscience. Guest starring Shannon Tweed

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Cheers owner Sam Malone turns up in Seattle to ask for advice on whether to go through with his wedding - but Frasier recognises his old friend's fiancee as a woman he slept with three months previously. Comedy, guest starring Ted Danson and Tea Leoni

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink casually wanders into Daphne's bedroom, making her furious with him. He apologises, but inadvertently does it again and she threatens to leave, forcing him to shower her with gifts to make her stay. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.desc: French amateur cook Aude Dupont-Dudley serves up her authentic Provençal squid stew, so chef Frederick Forster hits back with his family's West African curry

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer finds himself caught in the throes of a mid-life crisis, so he takes up bass guitar and forms a band with some of the other dads in Springfield. However, things get awkward when their singer - none other than Apu - proves so successful that he quickly outgrows the rest of the group, leading to jealousy and resentment

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two sets of househunters whose searches were all about family. Retired couple Tom and Sue are living in their motorhome having sold their beloved cottage in Tom's native Yorkshire. The plan is to move 150 miles south to be near Sue's grandson - and they long for an open-plan home with a huge garden. Meanwhile, doting father Charlie, who is recently divorced, and his two children would like a new family home in Kent to start a new chapter in their lives

programme.title: A Very Royal Baby: From Cradle to Crown
programme.desc: As the world awaits the imminent arrival of Harry and Meghan's second child, this documentary celebrates what it really means to be born royal. A combination of archive footage and interviews provides an insight into every stage of the royal baby story, looking at the pressures faced by royal mums-to-be and how attitudes have changed over the past 100 years. How does press intrusion compares with the birth of Meghan's babies? Why have the royals moved from home to private hospitals? And what really happened at Meghan's baby shower?

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: The launch of the site is just days away, but Johnny's last-minute design changes for his Maltese bus, including hand painting more than 200 tiles for the shower, are driving craftsman Pauleo to despair. Meanwhile, Bev is putting the finishing touches to the site up in Yorkshire before Johnny arrives to inspect the first of the vehicles, , including a converted American school bus and a beautifully restored Morris Minor Caravanette

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A romp through the archive of the unconventional dating show, beginning with assorted 'oh my god!' moments, and catching up with some of the most famous participants. They include Judith, the breast cancer survivor from series five who played The Lord Is My Shepherd to the potential dates and made them a Victoria sponge

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In east Lancashire, young entrepreneur Ellie and her drama teacher partner Andy kick off the competition with an oriental-themed menu. Day two sees stylish couple Jon and his partner Chris lay on a Mediterranean menu, with influence from their mothers. Finally, Gladys and her hubby Stephen are able to show off their passion for travel with a global menu, though there's a surprise in store for their guests when Stephen makes a revelation about his wife

programme.title: Devil's Knot (2013)
programme.desc: Three teenagers are accused of murdering children as part of a Satanic ritual. A private investigator hired to assist the court case begins to suspect they are the victims of a witch hunt, while the mother of one of the dead boys is torn between her suspicions over the lack of evidence and her need for closure. Fact-based crime drama, starring Reese Witherspoon and Colin Firth

programme.title: The Great Garden Revolution
programme.desc: Joel Bird creates an incredible teaching shed with a front that's covered in habitats for wildlife, while Poppy Okotcha creates sensory beds and vertical planting that's perfect for any smaller garden. The team also visit the Hanifs, who live in the middle of Birmingham, but want to create a forest school that the whole community can benefit from

programme.title: The £100k Drop

programme.desc: Andy, the ex-convict who once tried to murder Diane, makes an unwelcome return, sending her natural paranoia into overdrive. Comedy, starring Shelley Long and Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane borrows $500 from Sam to buy a book signed by Ernest Hemingway and allows him to look after it as security for the money - but things do not go as planned

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra cannot understand why Ray keeps turning down her advances - not realising that playing it cool is all part of his latest devious plan to get what he wants. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Pat asks Robert to keep her cigarette habit a secret - but he later has to come up with an excuse when Amy smells smoke on him, leading to a string of revelations for the family

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: In the last-ever episode of the American comedy, Ray has a brush with death while undergoing minor surgery, but pulls through - leaving his family uncertain over whether to tell him what happened

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Crane brothers are both invited to join a prestigious gentlemen's club - but unfortunately there is only a single vacancy available, meaning one of them is destined for disappointment. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink worries he is being stalked by an unbalanced fan after receiving strange gifts with seemingly threatening messages attached. In an effort to thwart any assassination attempt he hires a female bodyguard to protect him at an awards ceremony. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Worried that Martin has never expressed his love for them, Frasier and Niles join their father on a fishing trip in the hope that they can bond with him. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, David Hyde Pierce and John Mahoney

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Arthur race to collect low-hanging fruit to make delicious redcurrant jelly. In south-west France, Donna and Paul are hard at work on their most ambitious project yet, creating a tea room. However, stringent building regulations add to the challenge. Elsewhere, Ithaca and Alex need to raise some cash to help repair their chateau's 300-year-old roof

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples compete in Plymouth. The first hosts, Mena and her husband Chris, put together a locally sourced menu, and a boogie on the piano impresses their guests. Lovebirds Vicky and her boyfriend Chris host next, requesting everyone to come dressed as a famous couple. The final hosts are seafaring married couple Sarah and Chris, whose guests make a gallant effort with their costumes for a nautical evening

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch

programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with sports broadcaster John Inverdale joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Dartmoor couple Maria and Andy met on Cyprus 20 years ago, got married there and now plan to live there permanently. They're particularly fond of Famagusta, and want a home that will work for them and their much-loved cat Keo. With their budget of £140,000, property expert Jean Johansson lines up five stunning homes for them to choose between

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests check in to the Preston Hotel in Yeovil, owned by father and son Bis and Deep Oozageer. Ann and Rebecca find their room more cheap than cheerful, although an afternoon tasting the produce at a cider brandy distillery proves a massive hit

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Amateur cook Filippo Adamo is keen to tantalise taste buds with his traditional creamy mushroom risotto. Chef Mark Sargeant aims to beat Filippo at his own game when they knock up some gnocchi. Andi Oliver hosts as the pair compete in the fast-paced cook-off

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer gets into the spirit of the season during a Christmas Eve session at Moe's, and after getting a tad too merry he is told not to come home by an angry Marge. Wandering around Springfield all on his own, the lovable oaf is left to think about his actions - but will he see the error of his ways?

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Flashbacks reveal new information about George's final moments. Darren decides to tell Nancy how he feels, and Cher continues to gaslight Mercedes

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.desc: Excitable French bulldog Buddy arrives at the centre. His owners have tried selling him seven times but every time he has been rejected. Can the staff finally find him a forever home? An eight-year-old girl who always wanted a small dog has her mind changed when she sees stray labrador Leah, and nervous collie Kimmie needs another dog to keep her company

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: In the series grand final, Charlotte Ritchie stumbles into some sand, Lee Mack dunks Little Alex Horne and Sarah Kendall stacks fish fingers. Meanwhile, Jamali Maddix staggers around a bit and Mike Wozniak staggers absolutely everyone - again - all in a bid to secure the most coveted prize in television - Greg Davies' golden head nestled on a spike

programme.title: We Are Lady Parts
programme.desc: Comedy charting the highs and lows of Muslim punk band Lady Parts, as seen through the eyes of Amina Hussain - a geeky microbiology PhD student, who is recruited to be their unlikely lead guitarist at the instigation of fiery frontwoman Saira, who sees something in Amina that the others cannot. Amina is torn between two different worlds and on the lookout for love, romance - and a husband. Anjana Vasan, Sarah Kameela Impey, Juliette Motamed and Lucie Shorthouse star

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including SAS: Who Dares Wins, Dragons' Den, Three Families, Headspace: Guide to Sleep, Secret Crush, Lego Masters USA and This Morning. They also proffer their thoughts on a BBC News report about taking holidays abroad as the pandemic rules on travel are relaxed

programme.title: Tom Allen's Quizness
programme.desc: Contestants' general knowledge is put to the test as they play for the chance to win £25,000. However, the more answers the quizzers know, the dafter they must look to prove it. This first instalment features Dave, Hayley, Tharun and Saundra, who fight it out for a shot at the jackpot, facing tricky questions on anything from Captain Cook to Kelly Brook

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Pasadena, California, where he visits Jamaican-themed restaurant Cafe Kingston. The business in run by Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works at a radiotherapy clinic next door, and is determined to introduce hospital-style techniques to the kitchen by sealing everything in plastic bags

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: merican couples from different backgrounds temporarily switch spouses as they navigate emotive issues such as politics, class, gender and race with the ultimate goal of finding some common ground. In this episode, a professional wrestler swaps lives with a devoted wife and mother, whose disconnected husband prefers video games over family time

programme.title: How to Save a Grand in 24 Hours
programme.desc: Anna Richardson and the team give their expert financial, de-cluttering and DIY hacks to the Wallace family in Worcester, whose bank statements are not a pretty sight. They spend money faster than it comes in and mum Anna, who can't resist a bargain has a cosmetics drawer that rivals any professional make-up artist's. Dad Mark has fallen out of love with DIY and their youngest son refuses to eat the same evening family meal, which means hundreds of pounds is wasted on unnecessary cooking

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: With two thirds of all British Covid deaths coming after September 2020, Dispatches investigates why, and examines what role the Prime Minister's decisions of last Autumn played in there being a massive second surge in infections that saw tens of thousands of people die. As Boris Johnson's former chief advisor Dominic Cummings threatens to reveal all, reporter Antony Barnett hears from some at the heart of Downing Street discussions, and learns why the Prime Minister ignored government scientific advice and continued to battle against another lockdown. Dispatches also probes the Prime Minister's border plans to stop virus variants coming into the UK and hears from a whistleblower who fears they've failed to do the job

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: A report from Yangon, the largest city in Myanmar, as young protestors risk everything to defy the military junta's coup. As the death toll in the city mounts, cameras witness the early optimism and hope of a protest movement disintegrate as the security services launch a brutal crackdown on unarmed civilians. The Myanmar government says it is responding to protests that harm the stability of the nation, using non-lethal force. However, as pressure to release political prisoners and journalists intensifies, so does the bloody crackdown

programme.title: The £100k Drop
programme.desc: Davina McCall presents the quiz show in which Andy and Mel from Stockport and Bristol couple Alice and Wallis start off with £100,000 and are challenged to place quantities of the cash over trapdoors as they face a series of questions, the wrong answers to which will lose them money every time they slip up

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: Brendan takes the tourists sailing catamarans in the Spanish port of Vigo, where dolphins cause great excitement - but not as much as the arrival as two attractive new contestants

programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits the world's largest jelly bean producers to discover what they are made of instead of gelatine, and Matt Tebbutt finds out why maple syrup is so expensive

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: The broadcaster and guests discuss the issues of the day, with co-host Storm Huntley joining him for phone-ins and reading out viewers' correspondence

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Jean Robinson and Daithi Elliffe seek the help of architect Dermot Bannon to turn a listed 19th-century former schoolhouse at Kilternan, which has scenic views of the Dublin mountains, into their ideal family home. The property has been virtually disused for the past 30 years and is dank and dilapidated, with rotting floors, and the new homeowners are seeking a comfortable and bright dwelling with a modern layout. However, despite a generous budget of 360,000 euros, the constraints of a protected structure, a tight schedule and hidden costs begin to take their toll on the project

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.desc: Architect George Clarke meets a couple who want to move from Surrey to the Lincolnshire countryside, where they hope to start a business and convert a ruined 18th-century granary into an idyllic rural home

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: A near-miss with Terese makes Toadie and Melanie realise they are going to have to be much more secretive with their affair

programme.title: Killer Sentence: Hailey Dean Mysteries (2019)
programme.desc: Therapist and sleuth Hailey Dean knows District Attorney Paulina D'Orazio is innocent of the murder of a man she once put behind bars and must do all she can to prove it in the face of mounting evidence. Mystery drama, starring Kellie Martin, Viv Leacock, and Lauren Holly

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Bar manager Chris and hairdresser Gary have only been in Benidorm a week, but they have already found the perfect apartment and are now looking for work. But Chris has something else on his mind, which involves asking his boyfriend a special question. The documentary also catches up with Linda, who runs a dog rescue charity, and introduces Bradford-born Chris, who has turned his love of buying-and-selling into opening an antique and second-hand furniture shop

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: A near-miss with Terese makes Toadie and Melanie realise they are going to have to be much more secretive with their affair

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Christian finds himself drugged, strapped to a bed, and faced with a murderous Lewis. Bella tries to find a quiet space to finish her assignment

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: Dream Kitchens and Bathrooms with Mark Millar
programme.desc: The DIY expert comes to the aid of homeowners who are transforming the most important rooms in the house. In this episode, Mark meets Adam in Yorkshire, who wants to transform the bathroom in his rundown country cottage, featuring a vaulted ceiling, wood panelling, stone flooring and a vintage bath. Elsewhere in Walthamstow, Emma and Jono hope to create a Japanese-inspired designer kitchen, and turn to Mark for advice

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: A ewe in labour is rushed into the animal hospital from Cannon Hall Farm, and vet Matt prepares to deal with this urgent case in the car park. Meanwhile, Peter has a reunion with a feisty feline, and Morris the beagle is rushed into the practice by his worried owners after suffering a sudden illness

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: The Owen family try their hand at self-sufficiency with their very first dairy cow, and Reuben accompanies his mum and younger sisters to pick up Buttercup from a neighbouring farm. Back at the farm, the family take on the toughest job ahead of the imminent breeding season, rounding up the free-roaming ewes who have spread themselves across hundreds of acres of wild moorland

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben travels to the coastal forests of southern Sweden to meet 24-year-old single mother Annalisa, a native Italian who left everything in her home country behind when she was a teenager, and is now developing a forest hideaway on the fringes of society for her and her toddler. Ben helps her chop wood and make her home baby-proof, while Annalisa goes busking to make a small living. They also go dumpster-diving in search of rubbish that can be put to good use to counter the amount of waste thrown away

programme.title: Pompeii: The Bodies in the Basement
programme.desc: A new investigation into one of the most harrowing gravesites of the 79AD eruption, where more than 55 bodies were found cowering in a warehouse at a site that has long baffled archaeologists. While the skeletons at the back of the room have no possessions, those crowded at the front are laden with jewellery and gold - but why, in the chaos of their dying moments, were the bodies so starkly divided by wealth?

programme.title: Rogue Earth: When Mother Nature Takes Revenge
programme.desc: Using video captured on smartphones and other devices, this programme recounts the Super Outbreak of 2011, when 362 tornadoes ripped across 21 American states. The story is told by heroes, survivors and victims in Alabama, where 252 fatalities and millions of dollars in property damage occurred

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: How to Get a Good Night's Sleep
programme.desc: Part two of two. Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford continue their mission to cure their sleeping disorders. Eamonn is given a new sleep programme by top sports scientist Nick Littlehales, which requires that he sleeps in short bursts over 24 hours. Next, the couple investigate two luxury hotel 'sleep packages', before testing out a sleep pod designed to allow workers 20 minute naps

programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas teams up with a variety of well-known British stars to take an irreverent look back at the process of growing into adulthood. Suzanne Shaw and Megan McKenna offer up tales of embarrassing accidents up against a tree and behind a sofa respectively, while Ian 'H' Watkins explains how he made Gary Barlow suffer a similar misfortune. Plus, an account from a celebrity whose mother used their head to crack eggs, another whose treasure hunt unearthed a secret stash of condoms, and one who went to school with a boy nicknamed 'Hard-on Harvey'

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Paul Binski, Professor of the History of Medieval Art at Cambridge University, visits Chelsea's Gothic-revival Holy Trinity Church designed by John Dando Sedding, and William Lethaby's thatched All Saints' Church in Brockhampton, Herefordshire, which boasts fittings by William Morris and Edward Burne-Jones of the Arts and Crafts movement

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice helps a chauffeur and his girlfriend spruce up their neglected seaside bungalow in Clacton-on-Sea, Essex, to make it more attractive to potential buyers, transforming the interior and landscaping the overgrown gardens. Presented by Alistair Appleton

programme.desc: Holly visits the elves' workshop as they make toys for Christmas, but a problem arises

programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb investigates the origins of the obsession for mass witch-hunts and trials that swept the British Isles during the 17th century. Travelling to Scotland and Denmark, she challenges the accepted wisdom that King James VI of Scotland, later James I of England, was responsible for stirring up the hysteria. She also tells the story of Scotland's first mass witch trial in 1590 and the infamous Pendle witch trials towards the end of his reign

programme.title: April Jones: The Murder Tapes
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the murder of five-year-old April Jones in 2012, featuring previously unheard tapes of the 18 hours of interviews the police conducted with killer Mark Bridger, who was arrested within 24 hours of April being reported missing and charged four days later. The programme also features contributions from forensic psychologist Dr Joe Sullivan and April's family, who share their distress at her body remaining undiscovered
